name: Apple Raspberry Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
2. Freeze the flour in a medium bowl for one (1) hour. Refrigerate the lard for one (1) hour, then cut into cubes.
3. In a small cup, combine vinegar, salt, and water.
4. Measure 1 cup of flour onto a lightly floured surface; place lard cubes on top and cover with the remaining flour. Roll out the mixture until all pieces have been flattened and slightly incorporated.
5. Scrap into a bowl and mix with liquid.
6. Turn dough out onto a lightly floured surface. With a lightly floured rolling pin, roll out a rectangle about 8 X 12 inches. Fold to form an 8-inch square; fold again once more. Wrap in parchment or waxed paper and refrigerate until needed.
7. Peel and cut Granny Smith apples into eighths. Core them, then dice crosswise into 1½ chunks. Toss apples with lemon juice.
8. Blend together ½ cup sugar with cinnamon and cloves in a bowl.
9. Add apples and toss until evenly coated; add raspberries last and toss lightly.
10. Cut ⅓ of the dough and reserve. Roll remaining dough into a round about 5 inches larger than the dish, place it on top of the filling, and trim the overhang to one (1) inch.
11. Spoon any remaining sugar onto the crust; cut 5 slits in the center of the dough.
12. Bake at 425°F (220°C) for 10 minutes, then lower the temperature to 400°F (200°C) and bake for 40 minutes, turning occasionally until evenly browned. Let cool.
